prerequisites: An Oracle Database instance reachable from the connector (Airbyte tests against Oracle 11g, 12c, 18c, 19c, and 21c).
Network access from the connector host to the database listener, which uses port 1521 by default.
The database host address (host) and listener port (port).
A database user (username) with a password, granted CREATE SESSION plus SELECT on the tables or schemas you want to read.
The connection identifier for the connection_data field: either the database Service Name or the System Identifier (SID).
If using TLS encryption, the server's SSL certificate in PEM format.
If connecting through a bastion via tunnel_method, the jump server host, port, and OS login user, plus either an SSH private key or the SSH user's password.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a database username and password. The username is supplied in the non-secret username config field and the password in the secret password field. Create a dedicated read-only user (for example CREATE USER airbyte IDENTIFIED BY <password>; GRANT CREATE SESSION TO airbyte;) and grant SELECT on the tables or schemas to sync.

authMethods.name: TLS / SSL encryption certificate
authMethods.summary: When the encryption field is set to TLS Encrypted, provide the database server's SSL certificate in PEM format in the secret encryption.ssl_certificate field so the client can verify the encrypted connection. Native Network Encryption (NNE) and an unencrypted mode are the other encryption options.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel key authentication
authMethods.summary: Optional. When tunnel_method uses SSH Key Authentication, connect through a bastion host by supplying the SSH private key in the secret tunnel_method.ssh_key field, along with the jump server host, port (default 22), and SSH login username.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel password authentication
authMethods.summary: Optional. When tunnel_method uses Password Authentication, connect through a bastion host using the SSH (OS) user's password in the secret tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password field. This is the bastion login password, not the Oracle database password.

setupSteps.title: Create a database user and grant read access
setupSteps.body: In Oracle, create a dedicated user for the connector and grant it session and read access. For example: CREATE USER airbyte IDENTIFIED BY <your_password>; GRANT CREATE SESSION TO airbyte; then GRANT SELECT ANY TABLE TO airbyte; or grant SELECT on specific schemas or tables. Use this user's name as username and its password as password.

setupSteps.title: Provide host and port
setupSteps.body: Set host to the Oracle database server address and port to the listener port. The Oracle listener port defaults to 1521. Ensure the connector host can reach the database over the network (open firewall and VPC rules as needed).

setupSteps.title: Choose the connection identifier
setupSteps.body: Configure connection_data to identify the database by either Service Name or System Identifier (SID). A Service Name is a logical service that can route to one or more instances; a SID targets a specific instance. Use the value provided by your DBA.

setupSteps.title: Select an encryption method
setupSteps.body: Set the encryption field to one of: Unencrypted (plain TCP), Native Network Encryption (NNE), or TLS Encrypted. For TLS, supply the server's SSL certificate in PEM format in encryption.ssl_certificate.

setupSteps.title: Optionally restrict schemas and tune the JDBC URL
setupSteps.body: Set schemas to limit which schemas are synced (schema names are case sensitive and default to the uppercase username). Use jdbc_url_params to pass extra JDBC options as key=value pairs separated by & (for example defaultRowPrefetch=20).

setupSteps.title: Optionally connect through an SSH tunnel
setupSteps.body: If the database is not directly reachable, set tunnel_method to connect through a bastion host. Provide the jump server host, SSH port (default 22), and SSH login user, then authenticate with either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or the SSH user's password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). The SSH login user and password are the bastion OS credentials, not the Oracle database credentials.

sources.title: Identifying and Accessing the Database (Service Name vs SID, default port 1521) | Oracle Help Center
